topic: W/O-WINDOWS same-target flip check (OBJ-SIMPLIFY-TIMERS M1.G1 row 4)
verdict_detail: The Windows Instant/OrderedInstant runtime tournament (QPC vs the precise interrupt-time APIs InterruptTimePrecise / UnbiasedInterruptTimePrecise) selects the identical providers on the windows-2022 and windows-2025 GitHub-hosted runners at the same source revision. No frozen same-target flip, so the family freezes to a compile-time QPC pick in M2 (plan Section 5.2: same winner -> freeze QPC fixed).
notes: Both cells were dispatched in a single workflow run at the same SHA, so this is a same-source two-environment comparison (not cross-revision).

The windows-2022 cell also validated the CI test-robustness fix at 524b74a: five sleep-based elapsed tests previously failed on a loaded windows-2022 runner that overslept 5-10 ms sleeps to ~400 ms while tach stayed correct (elapsed_tracks_std_within_5_percent passed). SLEEP_ELAPSED_MAX_MS made those bounds robust.

The windows-2025 canonical cell's compose-speed.py step failed on numeric-cell validation against its frozen registry; that is orthogonal to the categorical provider selection compared here (its collector bundle uploaded via always() and its selection is identical).
